etymology.description: 朿(朿) (cì, 'thorn') duplicated -> suggests multiple thorns clustering in a tree
etymology.interpretation: The duplication of 朿(朿) (cì, 'thorn') visually suggests clusters of thorns or branches, which aligns with the form of a jujube tree where fruits grow densely amid thorny branches.
etymology.simplification: Simplified by reducing the duplication of 朿(朿) to a simpler form: 朿(棗) was replaced with ⺀(棗) to form the simplified 枣.

examples.chinese: 我每天吃一颗枣(我每天吃一顆棗)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ měitiān chī yī kē zǎo.
examples.english: I eat one jujube every day.

examples.chinese: 枣树生长在温暖的地区(棗樹生長在溫暖的地區)。
examples.pinyin: Zǎo shù shēngzhǎng zài wēnnuǎn de dìqū.
examples.english: Jujube trees grow in warm areas.

examples.chinese: 他喜欢用枣做甜品(他喜歡用棗做甜品)。
examples.pinyin: Tā xǐhuān yòng zǎo zuò tiánpǐn.
examples.english: He likes to use jujubes to make desserts.
